A large elliptical lesion, measuring approximately 70mm × 59mm × 61mm, is located in the right frontal lobe adjacent to the falx cerebri, with isointense signal in T1, slightly hyperintense signal in T2, and slightly hypointense signal in FLAIR. Within it, smaller nodular lesions, measuring about 23mm × 22mm, exhibit hyperintense signal in T1 and T2. The coronal section shows lobulated margins of the lesion, with overall clear boundaries. Adjacent brain parenchyma shows extensive hyperintense T2 signal due to compression. The splenium of the corpus callosum, bilateral anterior horns of the ventricles, and midline structures are displaced to the left due to the lesion. Post-contrast, the lesion demonstrates prominent homogeneous enhancement, with fine linear enhancement along the adjacent meninges. Additionally, a nodular-like enhancement signal lesion, measuring approximately 11mm × 6mm, is located adjacent to the right frontal lobe of the falx cerebri, and two smaller nodular enhancement signal lesions with indistinct margins are located adjacent to the posterior falx cerebri. The size and morphology of sulci, fissures, and ventricles are normal.
